title: euphrasia gibbsiae subsp. kingii
intro: Euphrasia gibbsiae subsp. kingii, also known as King's euphrasia or a species of the euphrasia genus, is a type of flowering plant commonly found in the Himalayan region and other mountainous areas. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits associated with Euphrasia gibbsiae subsp. kingii include its potential to treat eye problems, such as conjunctivitis and sties, as well as its use in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be applied to the affected area to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
